resources.string: Your conversations stay encrypted on this device
Are you sure you want to delete this conversation?
Start a conversation\nType a message below.
e.g. \'Be a friendly teacher who explains everything simply\' or \'Reply only in poetry\'
How to get a DeepSeek API Key

Go to platform.deepseek.com and sign up or log in.
Add to your account (top up) - this is a pay-as-you-go API.
Navigate to the "API Keys" section.
Create a new API key and copy it.
Paste the key above - it will be stored securely on your device.
Your key is only used to talk to DeepSeek, never shared anywhere else.
How can I help you today?
Delete \'%1$s\' and %2$d conversations inside? This cannot be undone.
No conversations here yet — long-press a chat to move it in
Your API key is encrypted with AES-256-GCM via Android Keystore
Your conversations are stored only on this device
Network requests go only to enabled providers — locked at the network security config level
No analytics, no crash reporting, no trackers
Get your free DeepSeek API key at platform.deepseek.com — it stays encrypted on your device
Get your %1$s API key at %2$s — it stays encrypted on your device
Optional — leave empty if your server doesn\'t require authentication
Enter your computer\'s IP address with port number
Type any model ID from openrouter.ai/models
Type the name of the model installed on your server
How to get a %1$s API Key
Private AI chat. Your key, your device.
Your API key is encrypted on your device
No trackers, no analytics, no data collection
Image is too large. Please choose a smaller image.
This provider doesn\'t support image input
File is too large (max 50 KB of text). Choose a smaller file.
API key not set. Please add your API key in Settings.
Could not connect to server. Check your internet connection.
Your API key was rejected. Check it in Settings.
Your API key has expired. Generate a new one from your provider\'s dashboard.
Too many requests. Wait a moment and try again.
Your API credit has run out. Top up your account at your provider\'s website.
The selected model is not available. Try a different model in Settings.
Your message is too long for this model. Try shortening it.
The AI provider is having issues right now. Try again in a few minutes.
The AI provider is overloaded. Try again shortly.
The request took too long. Check your connection and try again.
No internet connection. Check your Wi-Fi or mobile data.
Could not reach the server. Check your internet connection.
Could not connect to the server. The provider may be down, or check your server URL in Settings.
Secure connection failed. If using a local server, check your URL settings.
Unknown AI provider. Please reconfigure in Settings.
The AI returned an empty response. Try sending your message again.
This conversation could not be found. It may have been deleted.
Unexpected response format from provider. Try again or switch models.
Microphone permission is required for voice input
Voice recognition is not available on this device
Voice needs internet on this device
No voice available for this language
Hides app content from screenshots, screen recording, and recent apps
Your keys and chats are encrypted on this device. Maskan has no server and never sees your conversations.
When you chat with a cloud AI (OpenAI, Google, DeepSeek, etc.), your messages are sent to that provider and their privacy policy applies.
Prefer a cloud option? Venice is the most privacy-respecting — it doesn\'t store or log your chats.
For maximum privacy, use a local provider (Ollama or LM Studio): the AI runs on your machine and your messages never leave your network.
A private app is not the same as a private AI. Maskan keeps everything encrypted on your device, but when you send a message to a cloud AI, it has to reach their servers to be answered.
No Maskan server, no middleman. Your chats are never seen or stored by us or any third party.
API keys and conversations are encrypted on your device.
You choose where your messages go — including fully local, where nothing leaves your phone.
Works without Google Play Services or a Google account.
Open source (GPL-3.0) — anyone can verify these claims in the code.

It cannot make your messages invisible to a cloud provider you choose. If you use OpenAI, Google, DeepSeek, etc., they receive your messages and handle them under their own policies. No app can change this.
Where your messages go — most to least private
On your phone (Local) — Ollama, LM Studio
The AI runs on your own device. Your messages never leave your phone or network.
Your message is sent to Venice, but Venice says it doesn\'t store or log it.
Very private, based on their promise.
Standard cloud — OpenAI, Anthropic (Claude), Google (Gemini), DeepSeek, Groq, Mistral, Together, OpenRouter
Your message is sent to that company and handled under their privacy policy.

Tip: using these providers through your own API key in Maskan is usually more private than using the company\'s own app (like ChatGPT or Gemini), where chats are often used to train their models by default.
This image\'s contents will be sent to %1$s.
Voice is handled by your phone\'s system speech service, which may send audio to its provider (often Google).
